provider: HKU Faculty of Social Sciences official BSocSc Regulations and Syllabuses
note: The official 2025-26 BSocSc Regulations and Syllabuses publish the coded Politics and Public Administration first-major structure and seven optional second-major structures recorded here. POLI3154 is confirmed as a 6-credit course by the official HKU Department of Politics and Public Administration course list. Geospatial Data Science is excluded because its published structure contains placeholder GEOG4xxx course codes and remains pending approval.

supplements.programmeName: Bachelor of Social Sciences (Major in Politics and Public Administration)
supplements.ruleSummary: 96 credits: POLI1003 + 12 prerequisite credits from two other units; POLI2104 methods + two of four gateway courses; 30 credits advanced PPA electives; 6-12 capstone; 24 Social Innovation and Global Citizenship. Optional specialist stream requires 24 advanced PPA credits in one stream but is not recorded on transcript/certificate.
supplements.evidenceGap: The 12-credit prerequisite is expressed by unit rather than a closed coded choice list. Stream affiliations are deferred to the department website. POLI3154 is listed without a credit value in the official PDF; its 6-credit value is confirmed by the official department course list.

supplements.programmeName: Bachelor of Social Sciences (Major in Cognitive Science)
supplements.ruleSummary: 72-credit second major: PSYC1001 plus one of COMP1117/LING1000/LING2034/PHIL1012; 12 prerequisite credits from two listed units; PSYC2066 + PSYC2067; five electives / 30 credits from the published interdisciplinary pool; PSYC4068 capstone.
supplements.evidenceGap: The 12-credit prerequisite is expressed by unit rather than a closed coded choice list. The elective table omits repeated credit labels, but the rule explicitly defines five electives as 30 credits; each listed elective is therefore recorded as 6 credits from that official arithmetic.

courses.title: Advanced topics in reading, language and cognition
Mind and knowledge: An introduction to philosophy
Mind and language in Chinese thought
Bad language: the philosophy of non-ideal language use

supplements.programmeName: Bachelor of Social Sciences (Major in Counselling)
supplements.ruleSummary: 72-credit second major: choose one of SOWK1004/SOWK1012; 12 prerequisite credits from two listed units; all three 6-credit core courses; 30 elective credits from the three published groups; SOWK4009 capstone.
supplements.evidenceGap: The 12-credit prerequisite is expressed by unit rather than a closed coded choice list. The capstone is officially timed Year III or IV.

courses.title: Human behaviour and the social environment I
Communication and counselling skills in everyday life
Understanding and working with young people
Working with ethnically diverse communities in Hong Kong
Theory and practice in family counselling
Issues and interventions in mental health settings
Working with youth at risk and juvenile offenders
Counselling for emotional and relational competence
Integrative counselling methods in action: Theories, practice and research
Use of creative arts in social work practice
Frontiers in interventions around death, dying and bereavement
Encounter with embodiment: Therapeutic use of drama in social work settings
Cognitive behavioural therapy for common mental health 38 problems
Contemporary complementary and alternative psychosocial interventions for people with physical and mental health needs
Compassionate mind and practice: Paths to healing and growth
Experiential learning through the client’s lens

supplements.programmeName: Bachelor of Social Sciences (Major in Criminology)
supplements.ruleSummary: 72-credit second major: choose SOCI1001 or SOCI1004; 12 prerequisite credits from two listed units; SOCI2071 compulsory; 12 core credits with Group A/B rule dependent on whether Sociology is first major; 30 elective credits (minimum 18 Sociology + 12 other departments); SOCI4096 capstone.
supplements.evidenceGap: The 12-credit prerequisite is expressed by unit rather than a closed coded choice list. Law electives are subject to availability; LLAW1008 is encouraged preparation rather than a major requirement.

courses.title: Cross-border Legal Relations between the Mainland and Hong Kong
Rights and remedies in the criminal process
Introduction to sociology: The world’s on fire (and other problems)
Quantitative social sciences: From correlation to causality
Youth crime in the global city
Youth crime and juvenile justice issues
Working with youth at risk and juvenile offenders

supplements.programmeName: Bachelor of Social Sciences (Major in Media and Cultural Studies)
supplements.ruleSummary: 72-credit second major: choose SOCI1001 or SOCI1004; 12 prerequisite credits from two listed units; SOCI2080 compulsory; choose two core courses / 12 credits with at least one Group A; five electives / 30 credits, no more than two from one department except the 12-credit internship; SOCI4098 capstone.
supplements.evidenceGap: The 12-credit prerequisite is expressed by unit rather than a closed coded choice list. SOCI3104 is listed without a printed credit label but the core rule states each core course is 6 credits; internship eligibility requires Programme Coordinator approval.

courses.title: A dream in the heart: Varieties of Asian American culture
What’s on TV? Television and American culture
Sex, gender and modernity in China
The History of American Popular Culture
The History of Modern Sexual Identity and Discourse
Popular music: From Cantopop to techno
Introduction to sociology: The world’s on fire (and other 46 problems)
Media and culture in modern societies
Quantitative social sciences: From correlation to causality
China’s vision for humanity: Critical issues facing contemporary Chinese society
Selected topics in social and cultural anthropology
Hong Kong: Community and cultural policy in the global context
Media, culture and communication in Contemporary China
Sociology of knowledge, science and technology
Special topics in media and cultural studies
Anthropology of artificial intelligence: Human-like machine, machine-like societies
Capstone project in media and cultural studies

supplements.programmeName: Bachelor of Social Sciences (Major in Neuroscience)
supplements.ruleSummary: 72-credit second major only: PSYC1001; 12 relevant introductory prerequisite credits; all four core courses / 24 credits; 24 elective credits including at least 12 from Neuroscience electives, offered by at least two departments and including an outside-Psychology course; choose one 6-credit capstone.
supplements.evidenceGap: Prior Psychology Department approval and science background are required. The prerequisite selection is individualized and not a closed coded pool. PSYC4101 is a 12-credit major-only elective, not the capstone requirement.

courses.title: Stem cell biotechnologies in regenerative medicine

supplements.programmeName: Bachelor of Social Sciences (Major in Urban Governance)
supplements.ruleSummary: 72-credit second major: choose one of nine GEOG introductory courses in Years 1-2; 12 prerequisite credits from two listed units; choose three core courses / 18 credits; 30 elective credits across three themes; 6-credit capstone in Year 4, normally GEOG4001 with specified contingency substitutions.
supplements.evidenceGap: The 12-credit prerequisite is expressed by unit rather than a closed coded choice list. The PDF contains legacy/inconsistent Sociology codes and a truncated parenthesis for GEOG4004; these are retained as printed and should be reconciled against current course pages before verification.

courses.title: Hong Kong: Land, people and resources
Economic and social development in an urbanizing world
Human geography in a globalizing world
Modern maps in the age of big data
Cities and the urban world: A global introduction to urban geography
Political geography: International relations and the world economy
Introduction to research design in geography
Hong Kong’s environment: Issues and policies
Planning in an era of changes
The evolution of transport policy in Hong Kong
Climate change, air quality and environmental monitoring
Human-nature interaction for sustainability analysis and management
Sustainable land resource planning and management
Trees for green and liveable cities
Corporate social responsibility and environmental auditing
Exploring Korean cities and urban planning through K-movies and dramas
Studying cities: Quantitative methods for urban research
Urban development and planning: The Hong Kong experience
The Asian economic miracles and beyond
Social stratification and social class: Finding and knowing your place
Youth crime and juvenile justice issues
Community building and the civil society

supplements.programmeName: Bachelor of Social Sciences (Major in Psychology)
supplements.ruleSummary: 72-credit second major for non-BPsych students: PSYC1001 + PSYC1004; 12 prerequisite credits from two listed units; five fixed core courses plus one of PSYC2007/PSYC2051; 6 advanced elective credits; one 6-credit Advanced Lab capstone.
supplements.evidenceGap: The 12-credit prerequisite is expressed by unit rather than a closed coded choice list. The official course list includes the 12-credit PSYC4007 independent study although the second-major elective requirement is 6 credits; its applicability requires manual confirmation.
